semanticCore: Expulsion, banishment, curse, or deprivation of mercy
quranicRelevance: God's curse/banishment of Satan and wrongdoers (La'nah), and mutual cursing (Lian).

keyDerivations.meaning: To curse, banish, or expel from mercy
Curse, banishment, or exclusion from grace

figurativeOrRareUsages: الشَّجَرَةَ المَلْعُونَةَ (17:60): The cursed/banished tree (Zaqqum in Hell).
discardedNoise: Pre-Islamic social banishment rules for murderers.
Grammatical disputes on the passive participle form.
summaryEn: The root signifies banishment from divine grace, describing the spiritual expulsion of Satan and unrepentant wrongdoers.
summaryAr: يشير هذا الجذر إلى الطرد والإبعاد من رحمة الله، واصفاً الطرد الروحي للشيطان والظالمين المصرين على الذنوب.
rawText: لَعِينٌ The base, or lower part, of a raceme of a palm-tree. (TA in art. عهن.) لُعْنَةٌ A man who is much cursed. (TA in art. خدع.)
